entry.title: Bill 164 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2, ARTICLE 13, DIVISION 2, SECTION 2-69, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE HOUSING ADMINISTRATOR

Requires Council confirmation for the Housing Administrator.

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 164, Draft 2 passes first reading; adopts GOEAC-143 - 07/08/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 126 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLES 1, 3, 5, AND 7, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO SPECIAL DISTRICTS

Creates the Downtown Pāhoa commercial district as a new special zoning district; establishes new permitted uses.

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 126, Draft 3 passes first reading; adopts PCPLUED-12 - 07/08/26

View on the council site

entry.title: BILL018(26) (Honolulu): CCL/PH → PUBLISH
entry.content:
RELATING TO AFFORDABLE HOUSING.

Amends various development standards, building construction standards, and other requirements and benefits for affordable rental housing projects developed under ROH Chapter 32.

Status: CCL/PH → PUBLISH

Latest action: Second reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.

View on the council site

entry.title: BILL029(26) (Honolulu): CCL/PH → BUD
entry.content:
RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION.

Amends the requirements for filing a claim for a continued RPT exemption for low-income rental housing.

Status: CCL/PH → BUD

Latest action: Postponed to a date and time to be determined by the Committee Chair.

View on the council site

entry.title: BILL040(26) (Honolulu): CCL/PH → BUD
entry.content:
RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION.

Temporarily entitles the owner of certain owner-occupied condominium units to a real property tax home exemption. Applies retroactively to the tax year beginning July 1, 2025, and thereafter.

Status: CCL/PH → BUD

Latest action: Cancelled and not considered.

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 168 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed First Reading
entry.content:
REPEALS ORDINANCE NOS. 19-38, 09-50, 09-49, 06-138, 06-137, 95-118, 92-36, 90-10, 86-49, 533 (1980), AND 594 (1973) AND AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLE 8, SECTION 25-8-3 (NORTH KONA ZONING MAP) AND SECTION 25-8-5 (KAILUA URBAN ZONING MAP), OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM VILLAGE COMMERCIAL – 7,500 SQUARE FEET (CV-7.5) AND MULTIPLE

Director Initiated) (Area: 6,467 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation to revert the current zoning of 3.331 acres from Village Commercial to Double-Family Residential and 3.227 acres from Multiple-Family Residentia

Status: — → Passed First Reading

Latest action: LAAC-43: Recommended passage on first reading - 07/07/026

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 170 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPLUED: Bill 170 referred to Planning Director and Planning Commiissions - 07/07/26
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLE 1, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO ZONING

Distinguishes data centers from data processing facilities for zoning and land use purposes; defines “data center.”

Latest action: PCPLUED: Bill 170 referred to Planning Director and Planning Commiissions - 07/07/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 164 (Hawaiʻi County): updated
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2, ARTICLE 13, DIVISION 2, SECTION 2-69, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO THE HOUSING ADMINISTRATOR

Requires Council confirmation for the Housing Administrator.

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 154 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPLUED - Referred to the Planning Director and The Leeward and Windward Planning Commissions - 05/04/26
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLES 1, 5, AND 7, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO COMMERCIAL PARKING LOTS AND GARAGES

Removes commercial parking lots and garages as a permitted use in the general, village commercial, industrial-commercial mixed, limited industrial, general industrial, and Downtown Hilo commercial zoning districts.

Latest action: PCPLUED - Referred to the Planning Director and The Leeward and Windward Planning Commissions - 05/04/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 147 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPLUED: Referred to Planning Director and Leeward & Windward Planning Commmissions - 04/07/26
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 2 AND CHAPTER 25 OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO TRANSIENT ACCOMMODATIONS

Establishes operational standards and use regulations for transient vacation rentals, which include hosted bed and breakfasts (B&Bs) and un-hosted short-term vacation rentals (STVRs); allows B&Bs as a permitted use in the single-family residential zoning district; allows B&Bs as a permitted use in areas within the residential agricultural, family agricultural, and agricultural zoning districts that are also classified as State land use urban or rural; allows STVRs as a permitted use in the entire multiple-family residential district and in certain coastal areas adjacent to General Plan-designa

Latest action: PCPLUED: Referred to Planning Director and Leeward & Windward Planning Commmissions - 04/07/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 146 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the State Grants – Transit-Oriented Development Capital Improvement Program for 34 Rainbow Affordable Housing Project account ($1,000,000)

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 146 passes second & final reading - 05/05/26

entry.title: Bill 145 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – Housing Choice Voucher Program account ($1,000,000)

Latest action: Council: Bill 145 passes second & final reading - 05/05/26

entry.title: Bill 144 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
ADOPTS A PAY PLAN FOR PERSONS EMPLOYED BY THE COUNTY OTHER THAN THOSE WHOSE PAY IS OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR, DESIGNATING PAYROLL PERIODS, PAY DAYS, PRESCRIBING REQUIREMENTS WITH RESPECT TO OFFICIAL FIDELITY BONDS, AND REPEALING ORDINANCE NO. 25-39 AND ALL AMENDMENTS THERETO, SAID ORDINANCE TO BE KNOWN AS THE SALARY ORDINANCE OF 2026

Establishes rates of compensation for appointed employees in the Legislative Branch and the Office of Housing and Community Development, the County Physician, Student Helpers, and Senior Community Services Aids for various County agencies.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 144 passes second & final reading - 05/05/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 131 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO NEIGHBORHOOD COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CN-10) AT WAIĀKEA, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-2-034:045 (Applicant: CU

Hawaiʻi Federal Credit Union) (Area: 22,500 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to construct a 41-stall parking lot for the proposed new credit union facility to be located on Tax Map Key: 2-2-034:036. The property is located at 494 Hīnano Street.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 131 passes second & final reading - 04/08/2026

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 130 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 09-44, WHICH AMENDED SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2005 EDITION), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO NEIGHBORHOOD COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CN-10) AT WAIĀKEA HOUSELOTS, WAIĀKEA SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-

Hawaiʻi Federal Credit Union) (Area: 22,500 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this request to change of zone amendment for a ten-year extension to Condition C (Complete Construction), amendment to Condition D (Dedication of Road Widening), and deletion of Condition E (Roadway Improvements) which would allow the applicant to construct a new credit union branch facility. The property is located at 533 Manono Street. R

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 130 passes second & final reading - 04/08/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 141 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:

entry.title: Bill 140 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

entry.title: Bill 137 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-22 (PUNA DISTRICT ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL – 20 ACRES (A-20a) TO FAMILY AGRICULTURAL – 1 ACRE (FA-1a) AT ʻŌLAʻA, PUNA, HAWAI‘I, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 1-7-016:010 (Applicant

Benjamin and Olimpia Love) (Area: 5.79 Acres) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide the property into five lots. The property is located at 17-4050 Kuaʻāina Road.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 137 passes second & final reading - 04/22/26

entry.content:

entry.title: Bill 121 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 09-159, AS AMENDED, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM AGRICULTURAL – 5 ACRES (A-5a) TO MULTIPLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 30,000 SQUARE FEET (RM-30) AT KEAUHOU, NORTH KONA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 7-8-010:101 (Applicant

Kona Country Club, Inc.) (Area: 51.058 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested ten-year time extension to Condition D (complete construction). The property is located mauka of the Māmalahoa Highway Bypass, between the highway and the Kona Country Club mauka golf course.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 121, Draft 2 passes second & final reading - 02/18/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 120 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 04-149, AS AMENDED, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM AGRICULTURAL – FIVE ACRES (A-5a) TO SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 20,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-20) AT KALAOA 1ST AND 2ND, NORTH KONA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 7-3-011:068 (Applicant

Ernest and Elizabeth Young) (Area: 1.86 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested deletion of Condition H, which prohibits construction of a second dwelling unit on the property, and to amend Condition C for a five-year time extension to secure final subdivision approval. The property is located at the southwest corner of the intersection of Mahilani Drive and Māmalahoa Highway.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 120 passes second & final reading - 02/18/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 119 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 02-93, WHICH AMENDED SECTION 25-8-7 (NORTH AND SOUTH KOHALA DISTRICTS ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL (A-5a) TO RESIDENTIAL AND AGRICULTURAL (RA-2a) AT ‘ŌULI, SOUTH KOHALA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 6-2-007:002 (Applicant

Clemson and Janet Lam) (Area: 5 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested amendment to Condition D, which prohibits construction of a second dwelling unit on the property. The property is located at 62-2039 Kawaihae Road.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 119 passes second & final reading - 02/18/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 128 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 19, ARTICLE 6, SECTION 19-46.1, AND ARTICLE 11, SECTION 19-90, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXES

Adds a new “Residential Tier Three” real property tax classification for certain properties with a net taxable real property value of more than $4,000,000.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 128 passes second & final reading - 03/04/26

entry.title: Bill 124 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPLUED: Withdrawn - 06/16/26
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-26 (PĀHOA ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED) BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM VILLAGE COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CV-10) AND SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO DOWNTOWN PĀHOA COMMERCIAL (DPC) AT WAIAKAHIʻULA, PUNA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY THE AREA AS MORE PARTICULARLY DESCRIBED HEREIN

Rezones certain areas in Pāhoa to a prospective special zoning district classification to be known as the Downtown Pāhoa Commercial District, which is proposed for establishment via

Latest action: PCPLUED: Withdrawn - 06/16/26

entry.title: Bill 123 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:

Joseph Gaglione) (Area: 6.063 Acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this amendment to the State Land Use Boundaries Map for the requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide his property into two lots. The property is located at 62-2239 Kānehoa Place.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 123 passes second & final reading - 03/04/26

entry.title: Bill 111 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP) ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL – 3 ACRES (A-3a) TO SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) AT WAIĀKEA, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-4-080:014 (Applicant

Mailani Development, LLC) (Area: 4.4669 acres) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide the property into 14 house lots and a road lot. The property is located at 1077 Ahe Street.

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – Emergency Rental Assistance Program COVID-19 (Emergency Rental Assistance 2 Pass Through) account ($1,494,623.34) and appropriates the same to the Emergency Rental Assistance Program COVID-19 (Emergency Rental Assistance 2 Pass Through) account.

entry.title: Bill 88 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 10-32, AS AMENDED, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 7,500 SQUARE FEET (RS-7.5) AND MULTIPLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 1,000 SQUARE FEET (RM-1) TO MULTIPLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 1,500 SQUARE FEET (RM-1.5) AT KŪKŪAU 1ST, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEYS: 2-4-025:048, 053, AND 080. (Applicant

Ikaika Ohana) (Area: 5.05 Acres) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this request to amend Condition D (Complete Construction), which would allow the applicant to start construction no later than January 2026, with an estimated completion timeline of 18-24 months. The property is located at 350 Kapiʻolani Street.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 88 passes second & final reading - 11/5/2025

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 87 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Postponed
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 10-101, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM AGRICULTURAL – TWENTY ACRES (A-20a) AND SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 7,500 SQUARE FEET (RS-7.5) TO SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 15,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-15), SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10), SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 7,500 SQUARE FEET (RS-7.5), AND OPEN AT PĀHOA, NORTH KOHALA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEYS: 5-5-019:

Hawaiʻi Island Community Development Corporation) (Area: 18.38 Acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this request to amend Condition K (Affordable Housing) and Condition I (Fair Share Contribution), which would allow the applicant to utilize funds from the County’s Affordable Housing Production program to develop the final phases of the Kumakua Self Help Housing project. The property is located along the makai side

Status: — → Postponed

Latest action: LAAC: Postponed to the call of the chair - 10/7/2025

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 86 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Postponed
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-25, WHICH AMENDED SECTION 25-8-7 (NORTH AND SOUTH KOHALA DISTRICT ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL – 1 ACRE (A-1a) TO SINGLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) AT ʻŌULI, SOUTH KOHALA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 6-2-001:075 (POR.)

Hawaiʻi Island Community Development Corporation) (Area: 14.321 Acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this request to amend Condition M (Affordable Housing) and Condition N (Fair Share Contribution), which would allow the applicant to facilitate their ability to utilize funds from the County’s Affordable Housing Production program to develop the ‘Koaiʻe’ affordable housing subdivision. The property is located at the southwest corner of the

Status: — → Postponed

entry.title: Bill 66 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
ADOPTS THE COUNTY OF HAWAI'I GENERAL PLAN 2045 AND REPEALS ORDINANCE NO. 05 -025 AS AMENDED

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: PCPLUED: Bill 66, Draft 2 postponed to May 4, 2026 - 04/07/26 PCPLUED-10: Recommended passage on first reading - 05/04/26 Council: Bill 66, Draft 2 passes first reading; adopts PCPLUED-10 - 05/20/26 Council: Bill 66, Draft 2 passes second & final reading - 06/03/26

View on the council site

entry.title: BILL020(25) (Honolulu): CCL → MAYOR
entry.content:
RELATING TO FUNDS.

Creates a new Down Payment Loan Assistance Revolving Fund to receive and expend moneys to assist prospective homebuyers who reside in the City, do not own real property, and have an annual household income at 120 percent or below of the AMI of the City, as determined by HUD. Effective on July 1, 2025.

Status: CCL → MAYOR

Latest action: Deadline for Mayor to return Bill is 07/24/26. Transmitted to Mayor for his approval or otherwise.

View on the council site
